name: Session 2026-04-07

messages.content:
check if the workspace has:
- hacky code
- wrong design 
- wrong domain
- bug or error
- duplication
- improvement point

if it has any, fix and commit.
and repeat this step until you don't find anymore

I’ll do this as a bounded audit loop over the current workspace, excluding `.cozter` session metadata. I’ll prioritize concrete issues I can justify from code behavior or repo conventions, then build and re-scan before committing.

I found one real design/bug risk in the current fix: `StagedDirectoryCleanupGuard` owns cleanup responsibility but is still copyable and exposes its `active` flag. I’m going to make it a non-copyable RAII guard with an explicit `dismiss()`, and I’ll also rename the segment transfer overloads to explicit read/write helpers so the intent is not hidden behind overload resolution.

That local callback guard has the same shape and one extra behavioral edge: it is the owner of the project shutdown callback, so copying it would duplicate shutdown. I’m making it non-copyable with an explicit `activate()` while preserving the existing “call shutdown after startup was attempted” behavior.

Committed another cleanup pass.

Commit: `b86191a` `Tighten cleanup guard ownership`

Fixed:
- Made `StagedDirectoryCleanupGuard` non-copyable and replaced public `active` mutation with `dismiss()` in [filesystem.h](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/core/filesystem/filesystem.h) and [filesystem.cpp](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/core/filesystem/filesystem.cpp).
- Updated staged volume publish call sites, including [shader_asset_cooker.cpp](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/impl/assets_graphics/shader_asset_cooker.cpp).
- Made the loader’s project shutdown guard non-copyable and replaced public `active` mutation with `activate()` in [loader/main.cpp](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/loader/main.cpp).
- Renamed the filesystem segment transfer overloads to explicit `ReadSegmentBytes` / `WriteSegmentBytes`.

exts={'.h','.cpp','.hpp','.cxx','.c'}
issues=[]
checked=0
for p in Path('.').rglob('*'):
    if not p.is_file() or p.suffix not in exts:
        continue
    if any(part in skip_parts or part.startswith('build') for part in p.parts):
        continue
    checked += 1
    data=p.read_bytes()
    if data.startswith(b'\\xef\\xbb\\xbf'):
        issues.append((str(p),'bom','UTF-8 BOM present'))
    crlf=data.count(b'\\r\\n')
    lf=data.count(b'\\n')
    bare_lf=lf-crlf
    bare_cr=data.count(b'\\r')-crlf
    if bare_lf or bare_cr:
        issues.append((str(p),'line-ending',f'CRLF={crlf} bareLF={bare_lf} bareCR={bare_cr}'))
    if p.suffix in {'.h','.cpp'}:
        if not data.startswith(b'// limztudio@gmail.com\\r\\n////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\\r\\n'):
            issues.append((str(p),'banner','missing exact banner prefix'))
        if not data.endswith(b'////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\\r\\n\\r\\n'):
            issues.append((str(p),'eof','missing exact EOF separator/two blanks'))
for item in issues:
    print(f'{item[0]}: {item[1]}: {item[2]}')
print(f'checked files: {checked}; issues: {len(issues)}')
PY" (exit 0)

summary:
The user asked for an iterative local audit-and-fix loop: check the workspace for hacky code, wrong design/domain placement, bugs/errors, duplication, improvement opportunities, and violations of `standard.md`; fix and commit; repeat until no further issues. A standing constraint was maintained to keep `.cozter/sessions/*.json` out of commits.

The working tree started clean except `.cozter/sessions/c93aeca0e5be.json`, while `main` was already `ahead 10` of `origin/main`. The standards file was located at `.helper/standard.md` (not repo root). Audit scripts and targeted diffs were run against `origin/main..HEAD`, including style/rule probes and high-risk area checks (frame/platform code, Vulkan, filesystem, asset-domain validation, common/global headers). A key nuance discovered early: this repo intentionally uses CRLF and EOF blank-line conventions, so some `git diff --check` output against historical commits (e.g., “new blank line at EOF”) was interpreted as expected formatting, not defects.

Three concrete fixes were made and committed in sequence. First, direct `std::strcmp` uses were replaced with `NWB_STRCMP` in Linux frame code (`core/frame/frame_linux.cpp`, `core/frame/frame_linux_wayland.cpp`) to align with wrapper conventions; commit `b9d3e0d` (“Use project string compare wrappers”). During this edit, mixed line endings were introduced and then normalized back to CRLF before commit. Second, UTF-8 BOMs were removed from `global/name.h` and `global/name_pool.h` so files start with the required banner; commit `133b430` (“Remove source header BOMs”). Third, a missing standard separator block after includes was restored in `core/common/common.h`; commit `38a7cee` (“Restore common header section separator”).

Validation after each pass succeeded (`cmake --build --preset linux-clang-dbg --target testbed nwb_logserver nwb_resource_cooker`, later `linux-clang-fin`; `git diff --check`; additional repo-wide rule scans). Final state: no project-file diff remaining, only `.cozter` session metadata modified locally, and `main` is now `ahead 13` of `origin/main`. No open commitments remained.
long_term: Application entrypoint boilerplate should be shared via `global/application_entry.h` (`NWB_DEFINE_APPLICATION_ENTRY_POINT`) instead of duplicating `main`/`wWinMain` wrappers per executable.
Common subsystem initialization/finalization should use `NWB::Core::Common::InitializerGuard` from `core/common/common.h` rather than per-file local guard structs.

Vulkan pipeline creation paths should use centralized helpers for descriptor-heap/layout setup and owned-layout destruction in `vulkan_resource_bindings.cpp` instead of duplicating that logic per pipeline type.
Local `.cozter/sessions/*.json` is session metadata and should be excluded from commits unless explicitly requested.
The Linux final build preset in this repo is `linux-clang-fin` (not `linux-clang-final`).
Virtual-path override metadata validation should live in the assets domain via `Core::Assets::RejectVirtualPathOverrideField` in `core/assets/asset_paths.h` rather than in `Core::ShaderCook`.
Vulkan image-aspect mapping must preserve correct color/depth/stencil semantics and never include `VK_IMAGE_ASPECT_COLOR_BIT` for stencil-only formats.
Filesystem segment transfer helpers should be split into typed read/write paths (not a direction enum with nullable double-pointers), and `StagedDirectoryCleanupGuard` should own its log strings.
Repository coding standards are defined in `.helper/standard.md`.
Project `.h`/`.cpp` files outside `3rd_parties` are expected to use CRLF line endings, start with the `// limztudio@gmail.com` banner, and keep the standard EOF separator/blank-line convention.
When project wrappers exist for C-runtime string comparison, use `NWB_STRCMP` instead of direct `std::strcmp`.
